title.raw: 《田叔列傳》 Biography of Tian Shu

sentences.zh: 田叔者，趙陘城人也。

translations.literal: Tian Shu was a person from Xingcheng in Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu was a native of Xingcheng in the state of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 其先，齊田氏苗裔也。

translations.literal: His ancestors were descendants of the Tian clan of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: His forebears were descendants of the Tian clan of the state of Qi.

sentences.zh: 叔喜劍，學黃老術於樂巨公所。

translations.literal: Shu enjoyed swordsmanship, and studied the arts of Huang-Lao at the place of Yue Jugong.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu delighted in swordsmanship and studied the teachings of Huang-Lao philosophy under the master Yue Jugong.

sentences.zh: 叔為人刻廉自喜，喜游諸公。

translations.literal: Shu as a person was strict, incorruptible, and self-possessed, and enjoyed associating with various notables.
translations.idiomatic: By nature, Tian Shu was rigorously principled and incorruptible, taking pride in these qualities, and he enjoyed keeping company with distinguished men.

sentences.zh: 趙人舉之趙相趙午，午言之趙王張敖所，趙王以為郎中。

translations.literal: People of Zhao recommended him to Zhao's chancellor Zhao Wu, and Wu spoke of him to the King of Zhao, Zhang Ao, and the King of Zhao appointed him as a court gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Zhao recommended him to the state chancellor Zhao Wu, who in turn spoke of him to King Zhang Ao of Zhao. The king appointed him as a Gentleman of the Palace.

sentences.zh: 數歲，切直廉平，趙王賢之，未及遷。

translations.literal: After several years, being earnest, upright, incorruptible, and fair, the King of Zhao regarded him as worthy, but had not yet promoted him.
translations.idiomatic: Over the course of several years, Tian Shu proved himself forthright, incorruptible, and even-handed. The King of Zhao esteemed him as a man of worth, though he had not yet found occasion to promote him.

sentences.zh: 會陳豨反代，漢七年，高祖往誅之，過趙，趙王張敖自持案進食，禮恭甚，高祖箕踞罵之。

translations.literal: It happened that Chen Xi rebelled in Dai; in the seventh year of Han, the High Ancestor went to punish him and passed through Zhao, where the King of Zhao, Zhang Ao, personally held the serving tray to present food, his ritual courtesy being extremely respectful, but the High Ancestor sat with legs spread and berated him.
translations.idiomatic: It happened that Chen Xi raised a rebellion in the territory of Dai. In the seventh year of the Han dynasty, Emperor Gaozu set out to suppress him and passed through Zhao along the way. King Zhang Ao personally held the serving tray to present food to the emperor, his courtesy exceedingly respectful. But the emperor sat sprawled with his legs apart and berated the king.

sentences.zh: 是時趙相趙午等數十人皆怒，謂張王曰：「王事上禮備矣，今遇王如是，臣等請為亂。」

translations.literal: At that time, Zhao's chancellor Zhao Wu and dozens of others were all furious and said to King Zhang: "Your Majesty has served His Majesty with complete ritual courtesy, yet now he treats you like this; we your servants request permission to cause a disturbance."
translations.idiomatic: At this, the state chancellor Zhao Wu and some dozens of other officials were all outraged. They addressed King Zhang Ao: "Your Majesty has served the emperor with every proper courtesy, yet he treats you in this manner. We, your servants, beg leave to rise in revolt."

sentences.zh: 趙王齧指出血，曰：「先人失國，微陛下，臣等當蟲出。

translations.literal: The King of Zhao bit his finger until it bled and said: "Our ancestors lost the state; without His Majesty, we your servants would be like worms crawling forth.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Zhao bit his own finger until it bled and said: "Our forefathers lost the kingdom. Were it not for His Majesty, we would be no better than worms crawling from the earth.

sentences.zh: 公等柰何言若是！

translations.literal: How can you gentlemen speak like this!
translations.idiomatic: How dare you speak in such a way!

sentences.zh: 毋復出口矣！」

translations.literal: Do not let this come from your mouths again!"
translations.idiomatic: Never let such words leave your lips again!"

sentences.zh: 於是貫高等曰：「王長者，不倍德。」

translations.literal: Thereupon Guan Gao and others said: "The king is a man of virtue and will not betray kindness."
translations.idiomatic: At this, Guan Gao and the others said among themselves: "The king is a man of honor who would never repay kindness with treachery."

sentences.zh: 卒私相與謀弒上。

translations.literal: In the end they privately conspired together to assassinate the sovereign.
translations.idiomatic: In the end, they secretly plotted among themselves to assassinate the emperor.

sentences.zh: 會事發覺，漢下詔捕趙王及群臣反者。

translations.literal: It happened that the affair was discovered, and the Han court issued an edict to arrest the King of Zhao and the rebellious ministers.
translations.idiomatic: The conspiracy was eventually exposed, and the Han court issued an edict ordering the arrest of the King of Zhao along with all the ministers involved in the plot.

sentences.zh: 於是趙午等皆自殺，唯貫高就系。

translations.literal: Thereupon Zhao Wu and the others all killed themselves; only Guan Gao submitted to arrest.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wu and the other conspirators all took their own lives. Only Guan Gao submitted himself to arrest.

sentences.zh: 是時漢下詔書：「趙有敢隨王者罪三族。」

translations.literal: At that time the Han court issued an edict: "Anyone in Zhao who dares follow the king shall have their three clans punished."
translations.idiomatic: At that time, the Han court issued a decree: "Any person of Zhao who dares follow the king shall be punished together with all three branches of their clan."

sentences.zh: 唯孟舒、田叔等十餘人赭衣自髡鉗，稱王家奴，隨趙王敖至長安。

translations.literal: Only Meng Shu, Tian Shu, and more than ten others wore prisoner's red garments, shaved their own heads and placed iron collars on themselves, calling themselves slaves of the king's household, and followed King Ao of Zhao to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Only Meng Shu, Tian Shu, and some ten others donned the red garments of convicts, shaved their heads and clamped iron collars around their own necks. Calling themselves slaves of the royal household, they followed King Ao of Zhao all the way to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 貫高事明白，趙王敖得出，廢為宣平侯，乃進言田叔等十餘人。

translations.literal: When Guan Gao's affair was made clear, King Ao of Zhao was allowed to go free and was demoted to the Marquis of Xuanping; he then recommended Tian Shu and more than ten others.
translations.idiomatic: When Guan Gao's testimony cleared the king's name, King Ao of Zhao was released but demoted to the rank of Marquis of Xuanping. He then put forward the names of Tian Shu and more than ten others.

sentences.zh: 上盡召見，與語，漢廷臣毋能出其右者，上說，盡拜為郡守、諸侯相。

translations.literal: The emperor summoned and received them all, and conversed with them; none among the Han court ministers could surpass them. The emperor was pleased and appointed them all as commandery governors or chancellors of feudal kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned them all for an audience and conversed with each in turn. He found that none of the ministers at the Han court could surpass them in ability. Delighted, the emperor appointed every one of them as either a commandery governor or a chancellor to one of the feudal kings.

sentences.zh: 叔為漢中守十餘年，會高后崩，諸呂作亂，大臣誅之，立孝文帝。

translations.literal: Shu served as governor of Hanzhong for more than ten years. It happened that Empress Dowager Gao died, the various Lü clan members caused a rebellion, the great ministers executed them, and established Emperor Xiaowen.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu served as Governor of Hanzhong for more than ten years. Then Empress Dowager Gao died, and the Lü clan attempted to seize power. The senior ministers suppressed the Lü faction and placed Emperor Wen on the throne.

sentences.zh: 孝文帝既立，召田叔問之曰：「公知天下長者乎？」

translations.literal: After Emperor Xiaowen was established, he summoned Tian Shu and asked him: "Do you know who the men of virtue in the empire are?"
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Wen ascended the throne, he summoned Tian Shu and asked: "Do you know who the truly worthy men of the realm are?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「臣何足以知之！」

translations.literal: He replied: "How could your servant be sufficient to know this!"
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu replied: "How could your humble servant presume to know such a thing!"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「公，長者也，宜知之。」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "You, sir, are a man of virtue yourself, and should know them."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said: "You yourself are a man of worth. Surely you would know."

sentences.zh: 叔頓首曰：「故雲中守孟舒，長者也。」

translations.literal: Shu bowed his head to the ground and said: "The former governor of Yunzhong, Meng Shu, is a man of virtue."
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu pressed his forehead to the ground and said: "The former Governor of Yunzhong, Meng Shu—he is truly a man of worth."

sentences.zh: 是時孟舒坐虜大入塞盜劫，雲中尤甚，免。

translations.literal: At that time Meng Shu had been dismissed for the crime of barbarians making a great incursion past the frontier to raid and plunder, with Yunzhong being hit especially hard.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Meng Shu had been dismissed from office because barbarian raiders had broken through the frontier defenses on a massive scale, with the Yunzhong commandery suffering the worst of it.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「先帝置孟舒雲中十餘年矣，虜曾一人，孟舒不能堅守，毋故士卒戰死者數百人。

translations.literal: The emperor said: "The former emperor placed Meng Shu in Yunzhong for more than ten years; when the barbarians invaded in full force, Meng Shu could not hold the defense, and soldiers died in battle for no reason, numbering several hundred.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said: "The late emperor posted Meng Shu at Yunzhong for more than ten years. When the barbarians invaded in force, Meng Shu failed to hold his ground, and several hundred soldiers died in battle for nothing.

sentences.zh: 長者固殺人乎？

translations.literal: Does a man of virtue truly cause people to be killed?
translations.idiomatic: Does a worthy man send people to their deaths like this?

sentences.zh: 公何以言孟舒為長者也？」

translations.literal: On what grounds do you say that Meng Shu is a man of virtue?"
translations.idiomatic: On what basis do you call Meng Shu a man of worth?"

sentences.zh: 叔叩頭對曰：「是乃孟舒所以為長者也。

translations.literal: Shu knocked his head on the ground and replied: "This is precisely why Meng Shu is a man of virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu knocked his head upon the ground and replied: "This is precisely the reason Meng Shu is a man of worth.

sentences.zh: 夫貫高等謀反，上下明詔，趙有敢隨張王，罪三族。

translations.literal: When Guan Gao and the others plotted rebellion, a clear edict was issued from above: anyone in Zhao who dared follow King Zhang would have the three clans punished.
translations.idiomatic: When Guan Gao and the others plotted their rebellion, the throne issued a clear decree that anyone in Zhao who dared follow King Zhang Ao would be punished along with all three branches of their clan.

sentences.zh: 然孟舒自髡鉗，隨張王敖之所在，欲以身死之，豈自知為雲中守哉！

translations.literal: Yet Meng Shu shaved his own head and put on an iron collar, followed King Zhang Ao wherever he was, willing to die for him with his own body—how could he have known he would become governor of Yunzhong!
translations.idiomatic: Yet Meng Shu shaved his head and clamped an iron collar around his own neck, following King Zhang Ao wherever he went, willing to lay down his life for his lord—how could he have foreseen that he would one day become Governor of Yunzhong!

sentences.zh: 漢與楚相距，士卒罷敝。

translations.literal: Han and Chu confronted each other, and the soldiers were exhausted and worn out.
translations.idiomatic: While Han and Chu were locked in their great struggle, the soldiers had been worn down to exhaustion.

sentences.zh: 匈奴冒頓新服北夷，來為邊害，孟舒知士卒罷敝，不忍出言，士爭臨城死敵，如子為父，弟為兄，以故死者數百人。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu under Modu had newly conquered the northern barbarians and came to cause harm at the borders. Meng Shu knew the soldiers were exhausted and could not bear to issue commands, yet the soldiers vied with one another to mount the walls and fight the enemy to the death, as sons would for fathers and younger brothers for elder brothers, and for this reason several hundred died.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu under Modu Chanyu, having recently subjugated the northern barbarian tribes, descended upon the frontier to raid and plunder. Meng Shu knew his soldiers were utterly spent and could not bring himself to order them into battle. Yet the soldiers fought of their own accord, mounting the walls and throwing themselves at the enemy as sons would fight for their fathers and younger brothers for their elders. It was for this reason that several hundred perished.

sentences.zh: 孟舒豈故驅戰之哉！

translations.literal: How could Meng Shu have deliberately driven them into battle!
translations.idiomatic: How could Meng Shu have willfully driven them to fight!

sentences.zh: 是乃孟舒所以為長者也。」

translations.literal: This is why Meng Shu is a man of virtue."
translations.idiomatic: This is precisely what makes Meng Shu a man of worth."

sentences.zh: 於是上曰：「賢哉孟舒！」

translations.literal: Thereupon the emperor said: "How worthy is Meng Shu!"
translations.idiomatic: At this, the emperor exclaimed: "What a worthy man Meng Shu is!"

sentences.zh: 復召孟舒以為雲中守。

translations.literal: He recalled Meng Shu and again appointed him as governor of Yunzhong.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned Meng Shu back and reappointed him as Governor of Yunzhong.

sentences.zh: 後數歲，叔坐法失官。

translations.literal: Several years later, Shu was found guilty of a legal offense and lost his position.
translations.idiomatic: Several years later, Tian Shu was convicted of a legal offense and stripped of his post.

sentences.zh: 梁孝王使人殺故吳相袁盎，景帝召田叔案梁，具得其事，還報。

translations.literal: King Xiao of Liang sent people to assassinate the former chancellor of Wu, Yuan Ang. Emperor Jing summoned Tian Shu to investigate Liang, and he obtained full details of the affair and returned to report.
translations.idiomatic: King Xiao of Liang sent assassins to murder Yuan Ang, the former chancellor of Wu. Emperor Jing dispatched Tian Shu to investigate the affair in Liang. Tian Shu uncovered all the details and returned to deliver his report.

sentences.zh: 景帝曰：「梁有之乎？」

translations.literal: Emperor Jing said: "Did Liang do this?"
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing asked: "Is Liang truly responsible?"

sentences.zh: 叔對曰：「死罪！

translations.literal: Shu replied: "At the risk of death!
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu replied: "I deserve death for what I must say!

sentences.zh: 有之。」

translations.literal: It is so."
translations.idiomatic: It is true."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「其事安在？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "Where is the evidence?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked: "Where is the evidence of the case?"

sentences.zh: 田叔曰：「上毋以梁事為也。」

translations.literal: Tian Shu said: "Your Majesty should not pursue the Liang matter."
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu said: "Your Majesty must not pursue the matter of Liang any further."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「何也？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "Why?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked: "Why not?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「今梁王不伏誅，是漢法不行也；

translations.literal: He said: "If the King of Liang is not put to death, then Han law is not being carried out;
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "If the King of Liang is not brought to justice, it means the laws of the Han dynasty hold no authority.

sentences.zh: 如其伏法，而太后食不甘味，臥不安席，此憂在陛下也。」

translations.literal: But if he submits to the law, then the Empress Dowager will find no flavor in her food and no comfort in her bed—this worry would rest upon Your Majesty."
translations.idiomatic: But should he be made to face the law, the Empress Dowager will find no pleasure in her meals and no rest in her bed—and that burden will fall squarely upon Your Majesty."

sentences.zh: 景帝大賢之，以為魯相。

translations.literal: Emperor Jing greatly esteemed him as worthy and appointed him as chancellor of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing was deeply impressed by his wisdom and appointed him as the Chancellor of Lu.

sentences.zh: 魯相初到，民自言相，訟王取其財物百餘人。

translations.literal: When the chancellor of Lu first arrived, the people came to address the chancellor themselves, with more than a hundred suing the king for taking their property.
translations.idiomatic: When Tian Shu first arrived in Lu as chancellor, more than a hundred of the common people came forward on their own to bring suit, accusing the king of seizing their property.

sentences.zh: 田叔取其渠率二十人，各笞五十，餘各搏二十，怒之曰：「王非若主邪？

translations.literal: Tian Shu took twenty of their ringleaders and gave each fifty lashes with a rod, and struck the rest each twenty times, scolding them angrily: "Is the king not your lord?
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shu seized twenty of the ringleaders and had each given fifty strokes of the bamboo rod. The rest he had struck twenty times apiece. He rebuked them furiously: "Is the king not your sovereign lord?

sentences.zh: 何自敢言若主！」

translations.literal: How dare you presume to speak against your lord!"
translations.idiomatic: How dare you presume to bring accusations against your own lord!"

sentences.zh: 魯王聞之大慚，發中府錢，使相償之。

translations.literal: When the King of Lu heard this, he was greatly ashamed, and released money from the inner treasury, ordering the chancellor to compensate them.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Lu heard of this, he was deeply ashamed. He opened the inner treasury and ordered the chancellor to use the funds to compensate the people.

sentences.zh: 相曰：「王自奪之，使相償之，是王為惡而相為善也。

translations.literal: The chancellor said: "The king himself took their property, and now makes the chancellor compensate them—this makes the king appear evil while the chancellor appears good.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor said: "Your Majesty seized these goods yourself, yet now you order the chancellor to make restitution. This would make the king appear the villain and the chancellor the virtuous one.

sentences.zh: 相毋與償之。」

translations.literal: The chancellor should not participate in compensating them."
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor will have no part in repaying them."

sentences.zh: 於是王乃盡償之。

translations.literal: Thereupon the king himself repaid them all in full.
translations.idiomatic: And so the king himself repaid every one of them in full.

sentences.zh: 魯王好獵，相常從入苑中，王輒休相就館舍，相出，常暴坐待王苑外。

translations.literal: The King of Lu loved hunting. The chancellor would often follow him into the hunting park. The king would always dismiss the chancellor to rest at the lodgings, but the chancellor would go out and sit exposed in the open, waiting for the king outside the park.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Lu was fond of hunting. The chancellor would regularly accompany him into the royal hunting park, but each time the king would dismiss him to rest at the guest quarters. Instead, the chancellor would go outside and sit exposed to the elements, waiting for the king beyond the park walls.

sentences.zh: 王數使人請相休，終不休，曰：「我王暴露苑中，我獨何為就舍！」

translations.literal: The king sent people several times to ask the chancellor to rest, but he refused to the end, saying: "My king is out in the open in the park—why should I alone go to rest at the lodgings!"
translations.idiomatic: The king sent messengers several times urging the chancellor to go inside and rest, but Tian Shu refused every time, saying: "While my king endures the elements out in the park, why should I alone seek the comfort of a roof!"

sentences.zh: 魯王以故不大出游。

translations.literal: For this reason, the King of Lu no longer went on great hunting excursions.
translations.idiomatic: Because of this, the King of Lu greatly curtailed his hunting excursions.

sentences.zh: 數年，叔以官卒，魯以百金祠，少子仁不受也，曰：「不以百金傷先人名。」

translations.literal: After several years, Shu died in office. Lu offered a hundred gold pieces for his memorial shrine, but his youngest son Ren refused to accept them, saying: "I will not let a hundred gold pieces tarnish my father's name."
translations.idiomatic: After several years, Tian Shu died while still in office. The state of Lu offered a hundred pieces of gold for a memorial shrine in his honor, but his youngest son Tian Ren refused the gift, saying: "I will not let a hundred pieces of gold tarnish my father's good name."

sentences.zh: 仁以壯健為衛將軍舍人，數從擊匈奴。

translations.literal: Ren, being strong and vigorous, served as a retainer of the General of the Guard, and accompanied him several times to attack the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Ren, strong and robust in build, served as a retainer in the household of the General of the Guard, and accompanied him on several campaigns against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 衛將軍進言仁，仁為郎中。
數歲，為二千石丞相長史，失官。
其後使刺舉三河。
上東巡，仁奏事有辭，上說，拜為京輔都尉。
月餘，上遷拜為司直。
數歲，坐太子事。
時左相自將兵，令司直田仁主閉守城門，坐縱太子，下吏誅死。
仁發兵，長陵令車千秋上變仁，仁族死。
陘城今在中山國。
太史公曰：孔子稱曰「居是國必聞其政」，田叔之謂乎！
義不忘賢，明主之美以救過。
仁與余善，余故并論之。
褚先生曰：臣為郎時，聞之曰田仁故與任安相善。
任安，滎陽人也。
少孤貧困，為人將車之長安，留，求事為小吏，未有因緣也，因占著名數。
武功，扶風西界小邑也，谷口蜀道近山。
安以為武功小邑，無豪，易高也，安留，代人為求盜亭父。
後為亭長。
邑中人民俱出獵，任安常為人分麋鹿雉兔，部署老小當壯劇易處，眾人皆喜，曰：「無傷也，任少卿分別平，有智略。」
明日復合會，會者數百人。
任少卿曰：「某子甲何為不來乎？」
諸人皆怪其見之疾也。
其後除為三老，舉為親民，出為三百石長，治民。
坐上行出游共帳不辦，斥免。
乃為衛將軍舍人，與田仁會，俱為舍人，居門下，同心相愛。
此二人家貧，無錢用以事將軍家監，家監使養惡齧馬。
兩人同床臥，仁竊言曰：「不知人哉家監也！」
任安曰：「將軍尚不知人，何乃家監也！」
衛將軍從此兩人過平陽主，主家令兩人與騎奴同席而食，此二子拔刀列斷席別坐。
主家皆怪而惡之，莫敢呵。
其後有詔募擇衛將軍舍人以為郎，將軍取舍人中富給者，令具鞌馬絳衣玉具劍，欲入奏之。
會賢大夫少府趙禹來過衛將軍，將軍呼所舉舍人以示趙禹。
趙禹以次問之，十餘人無一人習事有智略者。
趙禹曰：「吾聞之，將門之下必有將類。
傳曰『不知其君視其所使，不知其子視其所友』。
今有詔舉將軍舍人者，欲以觀將軍而能得賢者文武之士也。
今徒取盎人子上之，又無智略，如木偶人衣之綺繡耳，將柰之何？」
於是趙禹悉召衛將軍舍人百餘人，以次問之，得田仁、任安，曰：「獨此兩人可耳，餘無可用者。」
衛將軍見此兩人貧，意不平。
趙禹去，謂兩人曰：「各自具樾象絳衣。」
兩人對曰：「家貧無用具也。」
將軍怒曰：「今兩君家自為貧，何為出此言？
鞅鞅如有移德於我者，何也？」
將軍不得已，上籍以聞。
有詔召見衛將軍舍人，此二人前見，詔問能略相推第也。
田仁對曰；
「提桴鼓立軍門，使士大夫樂死戰鬬，仁不及任安。」
任安對曰：「夫決嫌疑，定是非，辯治官，使百姓無怨心，安不及仁也。」
武帝大笑曰：「善。」
使任安護北軍，使田仁護邊田穀於河上。
此兩人立名天下。
其後用任安為益州刺史，以田仁為丞相長史。
田仁上書言：「天下郡太守多為姦利，三河尤甚，臣請先刺舉三河。
三河太守皆內倚中貴人，與三公有親屬，無所畏憚，宜先正三河以警天下姦吏。」
是時河南、河內太守皆御史大夫杜父兄子弟也，河東太守石丞相子孫也。
是時石氏九人為二千石，方盛貴。
田仁數上書言之。
杜大夫及石氏使人謝，謂田少卿曰：「吾非敢有語言也，願少卿無相誣汙也。」
仁已刺三河，三河太守皆下吏誅死。
仁還奏事，武帝說，以仁為能不畏彊御，拜仁為丞相司直，威振天下。
其後逢太子有兵事，丞相自將兵，使司直主城門。
司直以為太子骨肉之親，父子之閒不甚欲近，去之諸陵過。
是時武帝在甘泉，使御史大夫暴君下責丞相「何為縱太子」，丞相對言「使司直部守城門而開太子」。
上書以聞，請捕系司直。
司直下吏，誅死。
是時任安為北軍使者護軍，太子立車北軍南門外，召任安，與節令發兵。
安拜受節，入，閉門不出。
武帝聞之，以為任安為詳邪，不傅事，何也？
任安笞辱北軍錢官小吏，小吏上書言之，以為受太子節，言「幸與我其鮮好者」。
書上聞，武帝曰：「是老吏也，見兵事起，欲坐觀成敗，見勝者欲合從之，有兩心。
安有當死之罪甚眾，吾常活之，今懷詐，有不忠之心。」
下安吏，誅死。
夫月滿則虧，物盛則衰，天地之常也。
知進而不知退，久乘富貴，禍積為祟。
故范蠡之去越，辭不受官位，名傳後世，萬歲不忘，豈可及哉！
後進者慎戒之。
